name: Wellness/Habit Onboarding
definition_origin: Category pattern crystallized 2019-2024 by the wave of subscription-wellness apps that scaled past $100M ARR on the App Store: Calm (2012, $250M+ ARR by 2023), Headspace (2010, $300M+ ARR by 2024 + 'Headspace Therapy' launched 2025), Flo (2015, $100M+ ARR by 2024), Noom ($400M+ ARR before 2023 contraction), Cal AI (2024, $30M ARR Y1 — Henry Langmack & Zach Yadegari), Puff Count (2023, smoking-cessation app, hard paywall doctrine), Prayer Lock (2024, Mau Baron, $150K/mo by mid-2025). The dominant doctrine pairs the long questionnaire (Cal AI, item 17) or story-onboarding (Mau Baron, item 18) with an AI-personalized plan reveal (item 12) and a hard or soft paywall climax (item 34). Codified across Mobbin teardowns, Adapty 2026 wellness benchmarks, RevenueCat State of Subs 2025/2026 'Health & Fitness' segment data, and the Reforge / ProductLed 'Aha Moment' canon. Calm/Headspace popularized the 'gentle pacing' Caregiver register; Cal AI popularized the data-extraction Magician register; Mau Baron popularized the identity-led Hero register.
core_mechanism: Wellness/habit onboarding works because the category sells transformation, not utility — the user's purchase is an investment in a future self-concept ('person who meditates daily', 'person who tracks calories', 'person who quits smoking'). The onboarding flow must therefore produce identity formation BEFORE the paywall, which is why the long-questionnaire + AI-reveal pattern dominates. Five reinforcing mechanisms: (1) Sunk-cost commitment — answering 15-33 personalized questions makes abandoning feel like wasting one's effort (item 4 IKEA effect). (2) Identity confirmation — the AI reveal validates the user's self-concept ('You are a Type-3 sleeper who needs evening winddown — here's your plan'). (3) Pain-point salience priming — early questions about what hurts (sleep, anxiety, weight, smoking, prayer inconsistency) make the user re-experience the pain, increasing willingness-to-pay. (4) Social proof punctuation — '5M+ people like you' interleaved between question blocks reinforces the identity. (5) Peak-end engineering — the reveal screen IS the engineered peak (Kahneman) before the paywall closes. Wellness category specifically benefits because health/spiritual identity is high-emotion, high-LTV, and culturally validated as worth investing in.
neuroscience_correlate: Self-reference effect (medial prefrontal cortex, Kelley 2002) activates when the AI plan reveal references user-input identity. Anticipatory dopamine release (VTA → nucleus accumbens) at the loader-screen pause before reveal (Schultz 2016 reward prediction error). Anxiolytic activation in mindfulness/meditation onboarding via vagal tone shift during demo breathing exercises (Tang et al. 2015 Nature Reviews Neuroscience). Identity-formation engages dorsomedial PFC and posterior cingulate (Northoff & Bermpohl 2004). Pain-salience priming activates anterior cingulate during recall of suffering (Shackman et al. 2011), which biases willingness-to-pay upward.
empirical_evidence: Cal AI: 33-step questionnaire onboarding → AI macro-plan reveal → hard paywall = $30M ARR Y1 (founder interview, August 2024 — Zach Yadegari & Henry Langmack on Lenny's Newsletter; SimilarWeb 2024-2025 download data). Prayer Lock (Mau Baron): 5-min → 15-min story-onboarding extension TRIPLED organic conversion (Starter Story 2025; Mau Baron YouTube 2024-2025; case sample = single app). Headspace 2024 onboarding teardown (tearthemdown.medium.com): identity-led variant ('What brings you here?' → demo → personalized plan) hit ~70% completion vs ~40% for prior data-led version. Calm 2024: AI sleep stories (launched mid-2025) personalize at onboarding and re-engagement, ARR continued growth past $250M. Noom 2024: questionnaire + 'mid-onboarding review prompt' (Cal AI's same trick) drove 4.2-star App Store rating. Adapty State of In-App Subscriptions 2026: Health & Fitness onboarding paywall conversion median = 1.8% (vs cross-category median 1.35%); Health & Fitness 12-month LTV median = $58 (highest of any subscription category); Weekly + 3-day-trial paywall configuration shows 636% LTV lift vs no-trial in this category specifically. RevenueCat State of Subs 2025: Health & Fitness has highest D7 retention (24%) of any subscription category and highest annual-plan conversion. Flo: 200M+ users; questionnaire collects health/cycle data driving D30 retention; FTC settled with Flo in 2021 over Facebook data sharing — compliance precedent ($1.5M).
measurable_outcome_metric: Onboarding completion rate (target ≥60% for 8-15 min flow, ≥40% for 15-33 step flow). Paywall trial-start rate at climax (target ≥35% of completers; ≥1.8% of installs). D1/D7/D30 retention (D7 ≥20% category benchmark). 12-month LTV (target ≥$50). Trial-to-paid conversion (target ≥45%). Time-to-value (target: AI plan reveal under 10 minutes from install). Identity-question completion rate (each identity question target ≥85% completion). Mid-onboarding App Store review prompt acceptance rate (Cal AI ~30%).
conversion_lift_benchmark: Cal AI 33-step questionnaire pattern: $30M ARR Y1 from a single app. Prayer Lock 5→15min story extension: 3× organic conversion lift (single-app case study). Headspace identity-led vs data-led: +30 percentage-point completion (~70% vs ~40%). Adapty 2026 Health & Fitness: 1.8% onboarding paywall conversion (vs 1.35% cross-category); $58 12-month LTV median. Weekly + 3-day-trial: +636% LTV lift in this category. RevenueCat: D7 24% retention (highest of any category).
engineering_tactics: DEFAULT PATTERN: Story-onboarding (item 18, Mau Baron) for identity-led wellness (faith, meditation, journaling) — 5-act FSM affirm → social_proof → demo → personalize → ai_reveal → paywall, 12-20 screens, 8-15 min. ALTERNATIVE PATTERN: Long questionnaire (item 17, Cal AI) for metric-driven wellness (calorie tracking, fitness, women's health, smoking cessation) — 25-33 step quiz with chunked sub-progress ('Section 2 of 4 / Step 4 of 8'), AI plan reveal at step ~30, hard paywall at step ~33. Both patterns share: (1) AFFIRM act with pain validation in 1-2 screens, NOT a generic 'welcome to our app'. (2) Social proof punctuation after every 4-6 questions ('5M+ people like you reduced anxiety in 30 days'). (3) Identity questions only (item 27) — 'Are you a [meditator/runner/quit-smoker]' NOT 'What's your age?'. Demographics like sex/age allowed only when medically necessary (Flo cycle tracking, Cal AI BMR calc). (4) Cross-step stitching at reveal (item 14) — reveal headline must reference ≥3 user inputs verbatim. (5) Theatrical loader (item 13) 5-7 seconds with cycling copy ('Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you...'); LLM call must complete by then. (6) Mid-onboarding App Store review prompt (item 15) at peak emotional state, after reveal before paywall. (7) Paywall framed as 'lock in your [transformation]', NOT 'subscribe'. (8) Side-by-side plan selection (post-2026-01 Apple toggle-paywall ban). (9) Time-of-day routing (item 63) — surface evening calm flows after 8pm, morning planning before 10am. (10) Sensitive-data flag — health/biometric/cycle data triggers GDPR Article 9 special-category disclosures + COPPA persistent-ID disclosure (2025 amendments, 2026-04-22 deadline). FSM: XState v5 with persistence (MMKV on RN, UserDefaults on iOS). Server-driven UI (item 31) for A/B variant routing without App Store cycle.
real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI: 33-step questionnaire → 'Based on your goals (lose 10lb, run 3x/week, age 28F), your daily macros are 2,100kcal / 140g protein / 220g carb / 60g fat' AI reveal → mid-onboarding App Store review prompt → soft paywall with 3-day trial. (2) Prayer Lock: 12-screen story-onboarding → 'Your personal prayer for [reconciliation with father]' AI-generated full-text reveal stitched with user's prayer intentions → paywall framed as 'Lock in your daily practice'. (3) Headspace 2024: 'What brings you here?' (Anxiety/Sleep/Focus) → 60-sec demo breathing exercise INSIDE onboarding → 'When do you want to meditate?' → personalized starter plan (e.g. 'Sleep + Wind Down — 7 days') → trial. (4) Calm 2024: welcome → soundscape demo → 'What's your sleep struggle?' (chip-style) → personalized sleep-story plan → trial; AI sleep stories re-personalize at re-engagement. (5) Flo: cycle-length, period-length, height/weight, side-effects, conditions (PCOS/endometriosis) → 'Your fertile window prediction' AI reveal → mandatory trial commitment. (6) Noom: psychological-profile questionnaire ('How do you respond to stress?') → AI behavioral-coaching plan → mid-onboarding review prompt → soft paywall. (7) Puff Count: smoking-pain survey ('When did you last try to quit?', 'What pulled you back?') → personalized quit timeline → hard paywall (no skip). (8) Headway book-summary: identity ('What do you want to learn?') → 15-min book preview demo → personalized reading plan → paywall.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary. Wellness/habit category lives on phones — push notifications, daily check-ins, biometric integrations (HealthKit, Google Fit), Face ID for biometric gate, daily-streak haptics. Web equivalent (Calm.com web checkout, Noom desktop) is conversion-significantly-lower; web is reserved for upper-funnel SEO + checkout-only flows. Adapty 2026: web checkout for wellness apps captures only 8-15% of subscriptions despite Apple/Epic ruling enabling it. Mau Baron quote: 'Wellness lives on the phone — the web checkout is for the husband paying for his wife's app'.
platform_design_language_alignment: Strong alignment with Liquid Glass (iOS 26+, WWDC25): translucent material, bolder left-aligned typography is purpose-built for the AFFIRM act and reveal screens. Calm and Headspace 2025-2026 both adopted Liquid Glass elements rapidly. M3 Expressive (Android 16+, I/O 25): expressive shape system supports demo-act animations (Headspace uses M3E spring physics for breathing-exercise demo). Both DLs handle the theatrical loader (item 13) and ceremonial reveal screen natively. Wellness category specifically benefits because the calm-tech / soft-translucency aesthetic is on-brand.
category_fit: Strongest fit — this IS the category. Sub-segments: meditation/mindfulness (Calm, Headspace, Insight Timer, Healthy Minds), sleep (Calm, BetterSleep, Pzizz), women's health (Flo, Clue, Glow), calorie/fitness (Cal AI, MyFitnessPal, Lose It), smoking cessation (Puff Count, QuitNow), faith/prayer (Prayer Lock, Hallow, Pray.com), journaling/mood (Reflectly, Daylio, How We Feel), habit-tracking general (Streaks, Habitica), therapy (Headspace Therapy 2025, BetterHelp, Talkspace). EXCLUSIONS within wellness: B2B corporate-wellness (Wellable, Virgin Pulse) uses admin onboarding, NOT this pattern. Hardcore fitness (Strava, Whoop) uses minimal onboarding because users already self-identify.
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_month for full Cal-AI-style 33-step or Mau-Baron 5-act flow. Dependencies: Adapty Pro or RevenueCat (paywall infra), Plotline or Superwall (variant routing), OpenAI/Anthropic API ($0.01-$0.05 per AI reveal), XState v5 (FSM), Lottie/Rive (demo animations), MMKV (state persistence). Cal AI was built solo by Henry Langmack — proves solo-dev feasibility. Mau Baron Prayer Lock was built solo. The 1-month estimate assumes paywall infra + analytics already wired; otherwise 2-3 months.
tooling_options: Adapty Pro (wellness-leading paywall + variant orchestration; predominant in Health & Fitness segment per Adapty 2026), Superwall (paywall + onboarding A/B), RevenueCat (subscription infra + Customer Center), Plotline (visual onboarding flow + variants), Storyly (story-format screens native), OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini for AI reveal endpoint (frontier models — generic models produce flat reveals), Lottie / Rive (demo animations + theatrical loader), HealthKit (iOS) / Google Fit (Android) for fitness/sleep integrations, OneSignal / Plotline for push permission priming, Postmark / Resend for trial-reminder email pipeline.
ai_dependency: required (frontier model). The AI plan reveal at the climax is what converts identity investment into peak emotion. Use Claude Sonnet, GPT-4o, or Gemini 1.5+ — small models produce generic output ('Here's your meditation plan: Start with 5 minutes daily') that breaks the story. Cost: $0.01-$0.05 per onboarding reveal at scale; cache by similar input profiles. Latency budget: ≤7 seconds (matches theatrical loader). Offline fallback: pre-generated template plans keyed by archetype clusters.
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment: 'Build a wellness/habit onboarding FSM in XState v5 with 5 acts: AFFIRM (1-2 screens, pain validation — "Most people sleep poorly. We''re changing that."), SOCIAL_PROOF (1 screen, avatar grid "5M+ people sleep better with Calm"), DEMO (2-3 screens, live first-feature use — 60-sec breathing exercise WORKING in onboarding, not a video), PERSONALIZE (4-6 IDENTITY questions: "What''s your sleep struggle?" with chip options, NOT "What''s your age?"), AI_REVEAL (5-7s theatrical loader with cycling copy → LLM-generated personalized plan stitching ≥3 user inputs), PAYWALL (side-by-side annual/monthly, framed "Lock in your sleep practice"). Total 12-20 screens. Forbid: 33-step extraction without payoff; demographic-heavy openings; generic AI reveal that doesn''t reference user inputs; paywall framed "subscribe"; toggle paywall (Apple ban 2026-01); Inter font (use Söhne or Cabinet Grotesk); indigo-500 accent (use brand-defined OKLCH token). Persistence: MMKV. Server-driven variant routing: optional. Apple Liquid Glass alert sheets for paywall summary; M3E expressive shape for demo screens.' Second fragment: 'Build the AI_REVEAL screen — full-bleed gradient-free background, ceremonial typography (display weight 350, 56pt headline, line-height 1.05), prayer/plan stitched with user-inputs as bold tokens. Mid-onboarding review prompt (StoreKit) overlays at 4-second-dwell on reveal screen, before paywall. Forbid: hover:scale-105 on reveal CTA; emoji bullets; rounded-xl on every card; ring-1 ring-white/10.'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup a 12-screen wellness onboarding in story-arc form. ACT 1 AFFIRM (1) welcome with brand mark (no logo soup), pain-validation headline 56pt Cabinet Grotesk "Most nights, sleep slips away." Subhead 18pt Söhne "You can change that in 7 days." Single primary CTA "Begin". Off-white #F4EFE6 background, ink #1A1816 text. ACT 2 SOCIAL PROOF (2) avatar grid (16 photographic faces, NOT illustrated avatars) "Join 5M who sleep deeper" with single quote-card testimonial. ACT 3 DEMO (3-4) live 60-second breathing exercise with circular breath visualizer (NOT a Lottie of a demo — actually working). ACT 4 PERSONALIZE (5) "What''s your sleep struggle?" 5 chip-options (Anxiety, Restlessness, Late Bedtime, Early Wake, Mind Racing). (6) "When do you want to wind down?" time-picker. (7) "How long before bed?" frequency wheel. (8) commitment confirm. ACT 5 REVEAL (9) loader "Generating your sleep plan..." with cycling copy "Cross-referencing 2.4M users with similar patterns". (10) reveal full-screen — "Your Wind-Down Plan" with stitched user-inputs as bold tokens, ceremonial gold-accent (NOT purple gradient). (11) review prompt overlay (4-sec dwell). (12) paywall — side-by-side annual ($59.99/yr highlighted "Most popular") and weekly ($9.99/wk + 3-day trial). Headline: "Lock in your sleep practice". Forbid: Inter, indigo-500, purple-blue gradients, emoji bullets, max-w-7xl, rounded-xl on every card.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI typically (1) builds a 33-step questionnaire WITHOUT the demo act (skipping the load-bearing reciprocity move), (2) uses demographic questions (age range, gender as binary) instead of identity, (3) generates a flat AI reveal that doesn't stitch inputs ('Here's your sleep plan: 1) Wind down 2) Avoid screens 3) ...'), (4) frames paywall as 'Subscribe to continue' instead of 'Lock in your practice', (5) defaults to indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient on the reveal screen (peak AI-slop moment), (6) uses Inter font (defaults to wellness-app-as-tech-product instead of wellness-app-as-care). Counter-prompt: 'Build a wellness onboarding with 5 NAMED acts (affirm, social_proof, demo, personalize, reveal, paywall). The demo act MUST contain a live working preview of the core feature (not a Lottie video). The personalize act uses IDENTITY questions only — forbid age range, forbid binary gender unless medically necessary. The reveal MUST stitch ≥3 user inputs verbatim and use ceremonial typography (not gradient). The paywall headline must NOT contain the word "subscribe". Forbid Inter, indigo-500, purple-blue gradient anywhere. Use Cabinet Grotesk + Söhne, brand color in OKLCH.'
anti_patterns: (1) Story-as-decoration with no value delivered per act (demo skipped, just a longer Cal-AI extraction). (2) Demographic-heavy personalize act ('What's your age range?' opener). (3) Generic AI reveal ('Here are 5 tips: ...'). (4) Paywall framed 'subscribe' (breaks lock-in framing). (5) Toggle paywall (banned 2026-01 — Apple Guideline 3.1.2). (6) Aggressive sunk-cost extraction without reciprocity violates EU Digital Fairness Act. (7) Identity-formation pressure that excludes ('You are a runner' excludes wheelchair users — broaden to 'You are someone who moves'). (8) Cold native push permission dialog without priming (item 24/65). (9) Health-data collection without GDPR Article 9 consent. (10) Persistent-ID collection without COPPA consent for under-13s (Disney $10M Dec 2025 enforcement). (11) UGC creative misalignment — first 3 onboarding screens don't mirror the TikTok/Spark Ad. (12) Wellness app speaking in Outlaw or Hero voice when category demands Caregiver/Magician.
regulatory_risk: High. (a) GDPR Article 9 — health, mental-health, biometric, cycle data are special-category requiring explicit consent. (b) COPPA 2025 amendments — persistent IDs, behavioral, biometric data trigger VPC if mixed-audience. Flo FTC settlement 2021 ($1.5M) for sharing health data with Facebook is the precedent; Disney $10M Dec 2025 sets enforcement tone. (c) HIPAA NOT applicable to consumer wellness apps generally, but state laws (Washington My Health My Data Act effective March 2024) impose HIPAA-like duties on consumer health apps. (d) FTC Click-to-Cancel — wellness paywalls are FTC enforcement priority. (e) EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) explicitly targets manipulative wellness onboarding (sunk-cost extraction, fake urgency, addictive streak design). (f) UK Subscription Contracts Act 2024 — cancellation parity required from April 2026. (g) FTC enforcement against Noom 2022 ($56M settlement) for deceptive auto-renewal disclosure is direct precedent.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (subscription disclosure mandatory at paywall + 2026-01 toggle-paywall ban). Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization — collect only data needed for core function. Apple Guideline 1.1.6 (Health/Medical claims must be substantiated). Apple Guideline 5.1.1(ix) HealthKit data may not be used for advertising. Google Play Personal & Sensitive Information Policy — health data requires prominent disclosure + opt-in. Play Data Safety form must declare cycle/health/biometric data. ATT (App Tracking Transparency) — IDFA collection requires explicit prompt. iOS HealthKit requires NSHealthShareUsageDescription with category-specific copy. Android Health Connect (replaces Google Fit 2024+) requires explicit per-data-type permission.
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 — narrative arc helps screen-reader users. Demo act (audio breathing exercises in Calm, Prayer Lock) MUST have transcripts and alternate-input access (tap button replaces hold-and-breathe gesture). prefers-reduced-motion replaces theatrical loader with static text reveal. Dynamic-type / text-scaling required (SC 1.4.4) — wellness users skew older. 4.5:1 contrast minimum (SC 1.4.3) — common failure: cream-on-cream zen aesthetic violates. Touch targets 24×24 px minimum (SC 2.5.8, new in 2.2). Identity questions must avoid exclusionary language ('runner' excludes wheelchair users). VoiceOver/TalkBack tested copy (no emoji-as-button-label). Avoid motion-only progress indicators (SC 2.3.3). EU EAA 2025 enforcement applies.
pitfalls_caveats: (1) Reveal quality is everything — flat reveal collapses the entire arc; budget for human-edited LLM prompts. (2) Demo act requires real product code — adds 1-2 weeks dev time. (3) LLM cost at scale ($0.01-0.05 per user × 1000s of installs/day = real money); cache aggressively by similar profiles. (4) Latency at reveal — LLM call must finish in 5-7s loader window; fall back to template if timeout. (5) Localization — narrative bridges and identity language don't machine-translate; hire native copywriters per locale (Adapty 2026: localization 62.3% LTV win rate). (6) Time-of-day routing requires client-side hour detection + state-machine variant (item 63). (7) Mid-onboarding review prompt (item 15) requires careful timing — too early collapses, too late loses peak emotion. (8) UGC creative-onboarding alignment (item 42) is part of the doctrine — onboarding without ad-aligned welcome screen leaks conversion at the source. (9) FTC enforcement attention is high in this category (Flo, Noom precedents).
cross_cultural_validity: Strong fit globally with localization. JP/KR: Calm and Headspace localized to JP — short-form gentler narrative outperforms 15-min variants. MENA: Prayer Lock, Hallow, Muslim Pro lead — faith-led narrative culturally aligned. LATAM: aggressive paywall discount anchoring + monthly pricing wins (Adapty 2026); Cal AI variant 'Cal AI Latam' tested 50% off first month. CN: super-app context — short 5-8min variant outperforms; Calm closed CN ops, Headspace localized via Plumeria; native CN apps (Now冥想 by NetEase, Smiling Mind) dominate. RTL (Arabic): Prayer Lock RTL onboarding maintains all 5 acts. Color symbolism: avoid white-on-cream in mourning-symbolic markets (CN, JP, KR for very-pure-white).
prescribed_action: Use story-onboarding (item 18, Mau Baron 5-act) for IDENTITY-LED wellness (faith, meditation, journaling). Use long questionnaire (item 17, Cal AI 25-33 step) for METRIC-DRIVEN wellness (calorie tracking, fitness, women's health, smoking cessation). Both: (a) AFFIRM act with pain validation — NOT 'welcome'. (b) Live working DEMO act before any paywall ask. (c) IDENTITY questions only in PERSONALIZE — forbid demographic-heavy openings. (d) AI plan REVEAL stitching ≥3 user inputs verbatim, with 5-7s theatrical loader. (e) Mid-onboarding App Store review prompt (item 15) at peak emotion. (f) Side-by-side plan PAYWALL (post-2026-01 toggle ban) framed as 'lock in your [transformation]'. (g) Caregiver or Magician archetype voice — NOT Outlaw or Ruler. (h) Time-of-day routing for evening calm vs morning planning. (i) GDPR Article 9 + COPPA-amendment + Washington MHMDA compliance audit before ship.
questionnaire_relevance: PERSONALIZE act uses 4-6 identity questions (item 27) sequenced low-friction → identity (item 26). Cross-step stitching (item 14) at the AI reveal. Conversational form, one question per screen (item 28). Chunked sub-progress for 25-33 step variants (item 29).
aha_relevance: DOUBLE Aha — DEMO act is early Aha (live first-feature use, time-to-value < 90 seconds), AI_REVEAL is engineered peak Aha (Cal AI personalized macro reveal, Prayer Lock personalized prayer, Headspace personalized starter plan). The two-Aha compound is the dominant wellness onboarding pattern.
telemetry_events: onboarding_act_started, onboarding_act_completed, demo_act_engaged, demo_act_completed, identity_question_answered, ai_reveal_started, ai_reveal_completed, mid_onboarding_review_prompt_shown, mid_onboarding_review_prompt_accepted, paywall_shown, paywall_trial_started, paywall_purchase, push_primer_shown, health_data_consent_granted, gdpr_special_category_consent.
measurement_horizon: Day_7 retention as primary; Day_30 trial-to-paid; 12-month LTV (target $50+ benchmark). Day 0 conversion is necessary but not sufficient — wellness category lives or dies on D7-D30.
emotional_archetype_alignment: PRIMARY: Caregiver (gentle pacing, reassurance — Calm, Headspace), Magician (transformation arc, AI reveal as the spell — Cal AI, Prayer Lock). SECONDARY: Hero (the user's journey — Couch to 5K, fitness apps), Sage (wisdom-led demo — Insight Timer, mindfulness), Innocent (purity, optimism — meditation for kids, Smiling Mind). MISMATCHES to avoid: Outlaw (rebellion clashes with care register), Jester (humor undermines trust in mental-health), Ruler (control register feels coercive in habit-formation).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Frictionless Pattern (item 19) when category demands identity formation; against Cinematic 4-Screen (item 25) when category demands personalization. Pairs against Wall-of-Text + Cognitive Overload, Premature Authentication (don't gate the demo behind signup), Generic Celebration (mismatched archetype voice in reveal), Toggle Paywall (banned 2026-01).
sources: [Mau Baron — Starter Story Prayer Lock playbook (2025)](https://www.starterstory.com/mou)
[Washington My Health My Data Act (effective March 2024)](https://www.atg.wa.gov/my-health-my-data-act)
